title: Who With God Most High Finds Shelter

verses.text: Who with God Most High finds shelter, Lives in God Almighty’s shade. I will say to God, “My God, my Fortress where my trust is stayed.” From the fowler’s snare he’ll save you, From the deadly pestilence; Cover you with outspread pinions, Make his wings your confidence.

God’s truth is your shield and buckler; You will fear no ill by night, Nor the shafts in daylight flying, Nor disease that shuns the light, Nor the plague that wastes at noonday, At you side ten thousand fall; You will only see the judgement Which the wicked will befall.

You have made the Lord your refuge, God Most High your dwelling place. Nothing evil shall befall you; Near your tent no scourge you’ll face. He will angels charge to keep you, Guard you well in all your ways, Bear you up with hands that hold you, Lest your foot a stone should graze.

You shall trample serpents, lions, Tread on all your deadly foes. “Since he loves me, I will save him, Keep him, for my name he knows; I will honour, I will save him; When he calls, I will reply. I will show him my salvation, With long life will satisfy.”
